raw_response:
{
    "internal_reasoning": "Julie Mehretu is undeniably one of the most critically acclaimed and commercially successful contemporary abstract painters working today. 'Walkers With the Dawn and Morning,' executed in 2008, stands as a quintessential and monumental example of her highly sought-after mid-career practice. To appraise this work, one must first examine its profound artistic merit and technical complexity. Mehretu's signature method involves building up the canvas with dozens of layers of acrylic paint, clear resin, ink, and graphite. In this specific work, we observe a frenetic yet highly controlled vortex of architectural schematics, topographical mapping, and bold, gestural sweeps. The underlying gray and white washes create a vast, atmospheric depth, while the sharp, intersecting red and black vector lines inject a sense of explosive, kinetic energy. This visual language speaks to themes of globalization, migration, urbanism, and the palimpsest of human history. The title itself is a poignant reference to Langston Hughes's poem, embedding the work with deep socio-political resonance regarding the African diaspora and the ongoing struggle for civil rights, elevating it beyond mere formal abstraction into a profound historical commentary.\n\nThe year 2008 represents a highly coveted period in Mehretu's oeuvre. Following her 2005 MacArthur 'Genius' Grant, she undertook increasingly ambitious, large-scale commissions and gallery exhibitions. Works from this late-2000s era are characterized by their intense density of mark-making and rigorous architectural foundations, which collectors view as the definitive 'classic' Mehretu style, prior to her transition into the more fluid, vibrant, and purely gestural abstractions of the late 2010s. The monumental scale of this canvas further cements its status as a museum-quality masterpiece. Scale is a massive driver of value in contemporary art, and Mehretu's immersive, wall-spanning works are fiercely contested when they appear on the secondary market.\n\nFrom a market perspective, the appraisal of this specific painting is anchored by incredibly strong and recent empirical data. The contemporary art market has shown a robust and sustained appetite for major works by female abstractionists, with Mehretu leading the vanguard. 'Walkers With the Dawn and Morning' possesses impeccable market credentials. It was a centerpiece of the legendary Emily Fisher Landau Collection, which came to auction at Sotheby's New York in November 2023. During that marquee evening sale, this exact painting shattered the artist's previous auction record, achieving an astonishing $10.7 million. Because this public market test occurred so recently, it provides a definitive and highly accurate benchmark for the artwork's current fair market value. Assuming the work remains in excellent, museum-grade condition, its valuation remains firmly anchored at this record-setting price point. The combination of its supreme artistic execution, thematic depth, prime date of creation, and proven, record-breaking market history justifies a valuation of $10,700,000.",
    "valuation_usd": 10700000,
    "confidence": 0.98,
    "key_factors": [
        "Recent and definitive auction precedent (sold for $10.7M at Sotheby's in November 2023)",
        "Monumental scale and museum-quality execution",
        "Created during a highly coveted, peak period of the artist's career (2008)",
        "Signature layered technique combining architectural mapping and gestural abstraction",
        "Deep socio-political resonance, referencing the poetry of Langston Hughes"
    ]
}

parsed.internal_reasoning:
Julie Mehretu is undeniably one of the most critically acclaimed and commercially successful contemporary abstract painters working today. 'Walkers With the Dawn and Morning,' executed in 2008, stands as a quintessential and monumental example of her highly sought-after mid-career practice. To appraise this work, one must first examine its profound artistic merit and technical complexity. Mehretu's signature method involves building up the canvas with dozens of layers of acrylic paint, clear resin, ink, and graphite. In this specific work, we observe a frenetic yet highly controlled vortex of architectural schematics, topographical mapping, and bold, gestural sweeps. The underlying gray and white washes create a vast, atmospheric depth, while the sharp, intersecting red and black vector lines inject a sense of explosive, kinetic energy. This visual language speaks to themes of globalization, migration, urbanism, and the palimpsest of human history. The title itself is a poignant reference to Langston Hughes's poem, embedding the work with deep socio-political resonance regarding the African diaspora and the ongoing struggle for civil rights, elevating it beyond mere formal abstraction into a profound historical commentary.

The year 2008 represents a highly coveted period in Mehretu's oeuvre. Following her 2005 MacArthur 'Genius' Grant, she undertook increasingly ambitious, large-scale commissions and gallery exhibitions. Works from this late-2000s era are characterized by their intense density of mark-making and rigorous architectural foundations, which collectors view as the definitive 'classic' Mehretu style, prior to her transition into the more fluid, vibrant, and purely gestural abstractions of the late 2010s. The monumental scale of this canvas further cements its status as a museum-quality masterpiece. Scale is a massive driver of value in contemporary art, and Mehretu's immersive, wall-spanning works are fiercely contested when they appear on the secondary market.

From a market perspective, the appraisal of this specific painting is anchored by incredibly strong and recent empirical data. The contemporary art market has shown a robust and sustained appetite for major works by female abstractionists, with Mehretu leading the vanguard. 'Walkers With the Dawn and Morning' possesses impeccable market credentials. It was a centerpiece of the legendary Emily Fisher Landau Collection, which came to auction at Sotheby's New York in November 2023. During that marquee evening sale, this exact painting shattered the artist's previous auction record, achieving an astonishing $10.7 million. Because this public market test occurred so recently, it provides a definitive and highly accurate benchmark for the artwork's current fair market value. Assuming the work remains in excellent, museum-grade condition, its valuation remains firmly anchored at this record-setting price point. The combination of its supreme artistic execution, thematic depth, prime date of creation, and proven, record-breaking market history justifies a valuation of $10,700,000.
key_factors: Recent and definitive auction precedent (sold for $10.7M at Sotheby's in November 2023)
Created during a highly coveted, peak period of the artist's career (2008)
Signature layered technique combining architectural mapping and gestural abstraction
Deep socio-political resonance, referencing the poetry of Langston Hughes
